questions.question: The Montreal Protocol (1987) aimed to phase out:

questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer (1987) is a landmark treaty phasing out CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) and other ozone-depleting substances. It is considered the most successful environmental treaty.

questions.question: The Western Ghats is one of the world's 'Biodiversity Hotspots' because:
options: It has the largest forest area
It has exceptional species richness and endemism — over 5,000 species of flowering plants, 77% of amphibian species endemic — but faces severe habitat loss
It is the highest mountain range in India
It is the oldest geological formation
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats (UNESCO World Heritage Site, 2012) is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots. It has ~5,000 flowering plant species (1,700 endemic), 77% endemic amphibians, 32% endemic fish. Also home to tigers, elephants, lion-tailed macaque.

questions.question: The term 'ecological footprint' primarily measures:

The amount of land and water area required to support a human population or a particular activity.

The rate at which a species becomes endangered due to habitat loss.
questions.explanation: Ecological footprint quantifies the demand placed on natural resources by human activities. It represents the area of productive land and sea required to produce resources and absorb waste.

questions.question: The 'Tragedy of the Commons' best describes a situation where:
options: Private ownership leads to efficient resource management.
Individuals acting in their own self-interest deplete a shared, finite resource.
Government intervention always results in equitable resource distribution.
Technological advancements solve all environmental degradation issues.
questions.explanation: The Tragedy of the Commons illustrates how shared resources, when unregulated, are often overused and degraded by individuals seeking personal gain. It highlights the conflict between individual and collective interests.

questions.question: Which of the following international agreements primarily aims to protect the ozone layer by phasing out the production of ozone-depleting substances?
